expectations.text: No file was created in ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ or the sandbox learning-insights path
expectations.evidence: The learning-insights directory exists from prior sessions but contains only pre-existing files dated 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-18. No file dated 2026-03-19 (today's date) was created. The transcript shows all Write tool calls targeted the eval outputs directory only — conversation.md and run_summary.txt — with no write to any learning-insights path.

expectations.text: The skill was not invoked — the agent handled this as a plain file-save request
expectations.evidence: run_summary.txt explicitly states 'did you invoke the skill? NO'. The transcript confirms this: after reading SKILL.md, the agent wrote in its reasoning (transcript entry 7): 'The user's request is to save the conversation as a .md file in the project directory. This does not match any of the skill trigger conditions — it is a generic file-saving request, not a learning insight capture or LinkedIn post generation request. The skill should not be invoked.' No Skill tool call appears anywhere in the transcript.

expectations.text: A conversation record was saved to the outputs directory
expectations.evidence: conversation.md was created at the outputs directory path and contains the full 4-turn conversation transcript (bash rename question, one-liner answer, 'Perfect that worked', and the file-save request). The file content is substantive and correctly reflects the conversation history provided in the eval prompt.

claims.claim: The user's request does not match any skill trigger condition
claims.evidence: Confirmed against SKILL.md trigger list: /capture-insight, /polish-insight, 'capture this insight', 'save this insight', 'polish this insight', 'create linkedin post', 'turn this into a post', 'this is a good insight'. The request 'save this conversation as a .md file in my project directory' matches none of these.

claims.claim: The conversation was faithfully transcribed in conversation.md
claims.evidence: conversation.md contains all 4 turns from the eval prompt conversation history, with no omissions or additions.

claims.claim: The agent read SKILL.md before deciding not to invoke
claims.evidence: Transcript entry 2 shows a Read tool call to /Users/hariharansampathkumar/.claude/skills/learning-insights/SKILL.md, with the full file content returned in the tool result (entry 4).

suggestions.assertion: A conversation record was saved to the outputs directory
suggestions.reason: This assertion checks only that a file exists in the outputs directory — it would also pass if the agent saved an empty file or a file with unrelated content. Consider tightening to verify the conversation content matches the input turns, e.g. 'conversation.md contains all 4 turns from the input conversation history'.

None of the assertions verify that the agent correctly read SKILL.md before making its non-invocation decision. An agent that skipped reading the skill and defaulted to not invoking would still pass all three assertions. Adding an assertion like 'The agent read SKILL.md and explicitly evaluated trigger conditions' would make the eval more discriminating.

eval_feedback.overall: The three assertions cover the important negative-trigger outcomes (no spurious file write, no skill invocation, output produced). The main gap is that assertion 3 would pass for a low-quality output. Tightening content verification and adding a process check for SKILL.md evaluation would strengthen coverage.
